prose:
Electrostatics (dipole moment and Mulliken partial charges) — GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding) single point using xtb, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. 
Geometry provenance: fixed input coordinates from /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/electrostatics-validation/carbon-dioxide/carbon_dioxide.xyz; no geometry optimisation (electrostatics was evaluated directly, i.e. GFN2-xTB//input-XYZ).
Software/backend: xtb via chemkit electrostatics skill (version as bundled with chemkit; CLI in result JSON). Density-fitting is intrinsic to GFN2-xTB. 
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged (integrity.status = ok, trustworthy = true); no warnings returned; sum of partial charges (1.7×10⁻¹⁵ e) matches the net molecular charge within tolerance. 
Results: dipole magnitude = 8.0710129×10⁻¹⁵ D (expected ~0 D for a centrosymmetric linear CO₂); dipole vector ≈ (7.94×10⁻¹⁵, 1.40×10⁻¹⁵, 2.26×10⁻¹⁶) D. Mulliken charges: O₁ = –0.22459235 e, O₂ = –0.22459235 e, C = +0.44918469 e. 
Live log: the xtb standard output/err was streamed to a file electrostatics_⟨timestamp⟩.out in the working directory while the run executed (available for inspection alongside the JSON at /var/folders/ym/_02kbfyd29z17l376783nlm00000gn/T/tmplo5ujn7l/live.json).
